reasons: Executes arbitrary shell commands with shell=True which is vulnerable to command injection
Pushes code to git repositories (potentially production branches)
Uses environment variables for git config that could be manipulated
Function names suggest automated deployment workflow (build_commit_push)
No input validation or sanitization on command strings
Working directory can be arbitrarily specified
dangerous_operations: subprocess.run with shell=True enables arbitrary command execution
git config commands could be injected via environment variables
Automated git push to branches including 'main' (production)
5-minute timeout allows long-running potentially harmful commands
recommendation: Do not auto-deploy; this script requires manual review due to shell injection vulnerabilities and automated git push capabilities that could deploy untested code to production.
